title: ImageNet, an Image Database and Ontology :  History of Information
excerpts:
Section Title: ImageNet, an Image Database and Ontology
Content:
2008
Permalink
[Image Source: www.image-net.org](http://www.image-net.org/index)
In 2008 Principal Investigators [Li Fei-Fei](http://vision.stanford.edu/~feifeili/) of [Stanford Vision Lab](http://vision.stanford.edu/) and [Kai Li](http://www.cs.princeton.edu/~li/) of the Department of Computer Science at Princeton, and associates, advisors and friends, began building [ImageNet](http://www.image-net.org/index) , an image database and ontology, [through a crowdsourcing process](http://www.image-net.org/papers/ImageNet_2010.pdf) . In October 2013 the database contained 14,197,122 images, with 21,841 synsets indexed. Between 2013 when I wrote the entry, and September 2020 when I returned to, the number of images and synsets indexed remained unchanged, causing me to presume that this might be a static project.
The ImageNet database is organized according to the WordNet hierarchy.

title: About - Dr. Fei-Fei Li
excerpts:
Section Title: Dr. Fei-Fei’s thoughts on AI
Content:
I’m Dr. Fei-Fei Li, a computer scientist, AI researcher, entrepreneur, academic, and author. I am a cofounder/CEO of [World Labs](https://www.worldlabs.ai/) , a frontier model company focusing on solving spatial intelligence. I am also a professor at Stanford University, and founding director of [Stanford’s Institute for Human-centered AI](https://hai.stanford.edu/) (HAI).
I’ll be using this newsletter to share my thoughts and ideas about AI, and will be posting when I have updates to share.
Subscribe
[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!n-Jw!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6a294129-b40c-4f34-8759-eaa8e86cee82_2555x1789.png)

title: Fei-fei Li | Professor of Computer Science, Stanford
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Fei-fei Li
Content:
Professor of Computer Science, Stanford University
Fei-Fei Li is a Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University and Co-Director of the Stanford Human-Centered AI Institute. She is globally recognized for her pioneering work in computer vision, particularly as the creator of ImageNet, which helped catalyze the deep learning revolution. Li has served as Chief Scientist of AI/ML at Google Cloud, shaping its AI research and product strategy. Her research focuses on AI that augments human capabilities while aligning with ethical principles. She is a strong advocate for diversity in AI and co-founded AI4ALL, a nonprofit that expands access to AI education for underrepresented groups. Her work bridges cutting-edge research with societal impact, influencing global AI policy discussions.

title: The Godmother of AI | Princeton Alumni
excerpts:
Section Title: The Godmother of AI > The Godmother of AI
Content:
As the [Sequoia Capital Professor in Computer Science at Stanford University](https://profiles.stanford.edu/fei-fei-li) and co-director of the [Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence](https://hai.stanford.edu/) (HAI) — an interdisciplinary research hub that engages the social sciences and humanities — she’s spent more than 25 years doing just that while driving breakthroughs in cognitively inspired AI, computer vision, deep learning and AI systems for healthcare delivery. She is an elected member of the National Academy of Engineering, the National Academy of Medicine and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. In 2023, she was the recipient of the Intel Innovation Lifetime Achievement Award.

title: Fei-Fei Li is a Chinese-born American computer scientist best known for establishing ImageNet, the dataset that enabled rapid advances in computer vision in the 2010s.
excerpts: **Fei-Fei Li** ( Chinese : 李飞飞 ; pinyin : *Lǐ Fēifēi* ; born July 3, 1976) <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> is a Chinese-born American computer scientist <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> best known for establishing ImageNet , the dataset that enabled rapid advances in computer vision in the 2010s. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> She is a professor of computer science at Stanford University , <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> with research expertise in artificial intelligence , machine learning , deep learning , computer vision, and cognitive neuroscience . <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup>

title: An open letter to Fei-Fei Li concerning California’s proposed AI regulation, SB-1047
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Discussion about this post
Content:
It seems to be a favorite game of public figures ... write a piece for mainstream media righteously arguing for protection of a technology (or policy or whatever thing), gaslighting the reader with a twisted interpretation of a bill. Those who are good with words know words can be twisted into a rope you can hang just about any issue with.
Good on you Gary to keep them on their toes.

title: Fei-Fei Li: Human-Centered AI - Behind the Tech Podcast with Kevin Scott
excerpts:
Section Title: Behind the Tech ## with Kevin Scott > Fei-Fei Li: Human-Centered AI
Content:
**FEI-FEI LI** : Exactly. Test it yourself, just open your eye and close it. That will be longer than 150 milliseconds, but within that very short amount of time, the comprehension you have of the visual world is so rich. And that inspired my AI work because at that time, most of computer vision was still you know recognizing letters or --
**KEVIN SCOTT** : Oh, I remember.
**FEI-FEI LI** : Right? And -- or --
**KEVIN SCOTT** : And they were non-neural-network models and --
**FEI-FEI LI** : Right. Well, neural network was invented, in fact, my first course at Caltech was called Neural Networks. But it was working on very simple stimuli like digits and numbers. And then computer vision was still trying to understand edges.
